mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats that lift S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates expanding household balance sheets and wealth effects, which compound to drive positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional correlation with 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of rolling 3-year windows, or partial correlation of HOUST/RSAFS jointly becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, a rising industrial production index signals capacity utilization rebounding from slack, directly boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during INDPRO uptrends in post-shock periods, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds a 2.5% year-over-year growth threshold, inflation expectations rapidly embed into pricing models, forcing equity multiples to contract and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of triggered episodes, or the Rank IC between CPI inflation surprises and subsequent equity returns remains above 0.05 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth surprises, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand shock, which amplifies consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces negative or zero forward SPX returns in more than 45% of instances, or multivariate regression shows the joint coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts exceed 1200 thousand units, construction sector momentum and related supply chain activity accelerate, providing a near-term positive earnings catalyst that lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of cases where HOUST crosses above 1200, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 over a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation.
